heading: Right of States to enter and mark battle lines
label: 430p Right of States to enter and mark battle lines
text: It shall be lawful for the authorities of any State having had troops at the Battle of Monocacy to enter upon the lands and approaches of the battlefield for the purpose of ascertaining and marking the line of battle of troops engaged therein: , That before any such lines are permanently designated the position of the lines and the proposed methods of marking them by monuments, tablets, or otherwise, including the design and inscription for the same, shall be submitted to the Secretary of the Interior and shall first receive written approval of the Secretary: , That no discrimination shall be made against any State as to the manner of designating lines, but any grant made to any State by the Secretary of the Interior may be used by any other State.ProvidedProvided further
notes: Amendments1976— substituted “battlefield” for “Monocacy National Military Park” and “: for “, which approval shall be based upon formal written reports to be made to him in each case by the National Park Service:Pub. L. 94–578Provided further,”Provided,”.
amendments: Amendments1976— substituted “battlefield” for “Monocacy National Military Park” and “: for “, which approval shall be based upon formal written reports to be made to him in each case by the National Park Service:Pub. L. 94–578Provided further,”Provided,”.
